user_message: Hey, can you assess the risk for Coho Winery? Manager is asking for an update today.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Eric Kim <eric.kim@cohowinery.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-21 08:18 EST
Subject: Re: Re: QBR preparation Timeline

Just checking in on the QBR preparation proposal. Our VP Engineering is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
To: Eric Kim <eric.kim@cohowinery.com>
Date: 2024-02-27 09:53 EST
Subject: Re: Re: QBR preparation Timeline

Our team encountered some challenges with QBR preparation. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: Eric Kim <eric.kim@cohowinery.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-16 12:58 EST
Subject: URGENT: QBR preparation Issue at Coho Winery

Our team encountered some challenges with QBR preparation. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

Meeting: QBR preparation Discussion - Coho Winery
Date: 2024-02-16
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Eric Kim (Coho Winery), Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft), Kevin (Coho Winery)

[00:01:15] Eric Kim (Coho Winery): Appreciate everyone making time. The main agenda today is QBR preparation.

[00:06:00] Kevin (Coho Winery): Our VP reviewed this and has concerns about partner engagement. Can we address those?

[00:09:00] Eric Kim (Coho Winery): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is vendor dependencies.

[00:13:00] Kevin (Coho Winery): The timeline for resource allocation is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:18:00] Eric Kim (Coho Winery): On the QBR preparation front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around technical validation.

[00:21:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): Our VP reviewed this and has concerns about timeline constraints. Can we address those?

[00:23:00] Eric Kim (Coho Winery): On the QBR preparation front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around resource allocation.

[00:28:00] Eric Kim (Coho Winery): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is resource allocation.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF11-I trap (Over-summarizes -- loses specific recent context); produces complete One-page brief with attendee context, account snapshot, talking points, likely objections, open item
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF11-I failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF11-I failure mode (Over-summarizes -- loses specific recent context)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct QBR preparation decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF11-I -- Over-summarizes -- loses specific recent context
